categoryDescription.business: Business strategies, management frameworks, financial analysis templates, and operational know-how from experienced professionals. Find actionable insights for startups, enterprise operations, and career development.
categoryDescription.technology-it: Software engineering best practices, DevOps configurations, system architecture patterns, and AI/ML workflows. From production-tested prompts to infrastructure templates.
categoryDescription.design-creative: Design systems, UI/UX methodologies, creative workflows, and visual communication strategies. Practical knowledge from working designers and creative professionals.
categoryDescription.education-learning: Teaching methodologies, curriculum designs, learning frameworks, and educational technology guides. Knowledge for educators, trainers, and self-directed learners.
categoryDescription.lifestyle: Health and wellness practices, productivity systems, personal finance strategies, and life optimization insights. Practical knowledge for everyday improvement.

Footer.tagline: A knowledge marketplace for AI and humans

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.sec1Intro: This service collects and stores the following information.
Privacy.sec2Item3: Detection and prevention of fraud and security threats
Privacy.sec2Item4: Service quality improvement and feature development
Privacy.sec2Item6: Enforcement of Terms of Service violations
Privacy.sec3Body: This service uses cookies for session management. We may use Supabase's built-in analytics for access analysis. We do not share data with third-party advertising networks.
Privacy.sec4Intro: We do not share personal information with third parties as a general rule. Exceptions include:
Privacy.sec4Item1: When the user has given explicit consent
Privacy.sec4Item2: When required by law or legal process
Privacy.sec4Item3: When necessary to protect the life, body, or property of a person
Privacy.sec4Supabase: This service uses Supabase (a PostgreSQL cloud service) as infrastructure. Please refer to Supabase's privacy policy on their website.
Privacy.sec5Body: Personal information is stored in a database managed by Supabase and protected by Row Level Security (RLS). API keys are stored only as SHA-256 hashes; plaintext keys are never stored. Data that is no longer needed is deleted appropriately.
Privacy.sec6Item1: Right to access personal information we hold
Privacy.sec6Item3: Right to request deletion of personal information (except where retention is required by law)
Privacy.sec6Item4: Right to restrict processing of personal information
Privacy.sec6Closing: To exercise these rights, please contact us using the contact information below.
Privacy.sec7Body: We may revise this policy as necessary. If significant changes are made, we will notify users within the service. Continued use of the service constitutes acceptance of the revised policy.
Privacy.sec8Body: For questions or requests regarding the handling of personal information, please use our

Legal.title: Specified Commercial Transactions Act
Legal.description: Specified Commercial Transactions Act disclosure for KnowMint
Legal.value2: Kanagawa, Japan (disclosed without delay upon request)
Legal.value5: Prices are displayed in cryptocurrency (SOL, USDC, etc.) on each knowledge item's detail page. Fiat equivalents fluctuate with cryptocurrency market prices.
Legal.value6: Blockchain network transaction fees (gas fees) are charged separately. Fees vary depending on network congestion.
Legal.value7: Access to digital content is granted immediately after the payment transaction is confirmed.
Legal.value8: Cryptocurrency (SOL, USDC) payments via the Solana network. Support for EVM chains (Base, Ethereum) is planned.
Legal.value9: Due to the nature of digital content, returns and refunds are not accepted once content has been disclosed. Exceptions, such as when content significantly differs from the description, are detailed in the Terms of Service.
Legal.value10: Latest version of Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge, or other modern web browsers. A Solana-compatible wallet (Phantom, Solflare, etc.) is required.
Legal.value11: Text-based digital content (prompts, tool definitions, datasets, API specifications, general knowledge, etc.).
Legal.value12: This service is a digital content trading platform operated by a sole proprietor. Individual content sellers are registered users (sellers). The operator is the marketplace provider and not a party to individual transactions.
Legal.warningBody: Cryptocurrency payments carry price volatility risk. In the event of a transfer error, we are unable to issue refunds or compensation, so please verify the destination wallet address carefully before sending. This service is non-custodial; we do not manage users' private keys.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.intro: Please use the appropriate channel below depending on your inquiry. Response times may vary.
Contact.techDescription: Issues with login, payments not completing, content not displaying, or other technical problems.
Contact.contentDescription: Report content that violates terms of service, suspected copyright infringement, or inappropriate listings.
Contact.legalDescription: Seller information disclosure requests under consumer protection laws, personal data access/correction/deletion requests.
Contact.bizDescription: Partnership proposals, integrations, and other business inquiries.
Contact.dpfBody: For requests to disclose seller information under the Specified Commercial Transactions Act and consumer protection laws, please contact us through the Legal Disclosure channel above. Identity verification documents are required. We will respond within the scope prescribed by law.
Contact.faqPrefix: For general questions, please review our

Terms.title: Terms of Service
Terms.art1Body: KnowMint (the "Service") is a knowledge marketplace where humans can list and purchase implicit and experiential knowledge as digital content. The operator ("we" or "us") provides a venue for transactions between users, but the parties to each transaction are the seller and buyer; we are not a party to any transaction.
Terms.art2Body: Use of the Service requires the creation of a valid account. Please provide accurate and up-to-date registration information. Account management is your own responsibility, and any unauthorized use must be reported to us immediately.
Terms.art3Item2: Listing content that infringes the copyrights or intellectual property rights of others
Terms.art3Item3: Listing content that is illegal or contrary to public order and morals
Terms.art3Item4: Unauthorized access to or interference with the Service's systems
Terms.art3Item5: Money laundering or other illegal fund transfers
Terms.art3Item8: Unfair competitive practices conducted through the Service
Terms.art4Body1: Due to the nature of digital content, refunds will generally not be provided once content has been disclosed following purchase.
Terms.art4Body2: However, in the following limited cases, the seller and we will consult to determine an appropriate response.
Terms.art4Item1: The purchased content materially differs from the listing description
Terms.art4Item2: A technical issue prevents the buyer from accessing the content
Terms.art4Item3: The seller is confirmed to have provided false descriptions
Terms.art4Body3: Please note that canceling a transaction after a cryptocurrency payment has been completed is technically very difficult, so please review carefully before purchasing.
Terms.art5Intro: The Service supports cryptocurrency payments (SOL, USDC, etc.) using blockchain networks such as Solana.
Terms.art5Item1: Cryptocurrency prices can fluctuate significantly, and we do not guarantee the fiat-currency equivalent value at the time of purchase.
Terms.art5Item2: We bear no responsibility for payment errors caused by incorrect wallet addresses. Always verify the recipient address before sending.
Terms.art5Item3: Blockchain network outages, delays, and fee changes are outside our control, and we bear no responsibility for them.
Terms.art5Item4: Transactions are generally irreversible once the payment has been sent.
Terms.art6Body: We do not hold the private keys of users' wallets. Payments are direct peer-to-peer (P2P) transfers from the buyer's wallet to the seller's wallet. We only verify and record transaction hashes and do not manage or hold any funds. Users are solely responsible for managing their own private keys.
Terms.art7Body: Sellers warrant that they hold the necessary rights or have obtained the necessary permissions for any content they list. Buyers may use purchased content for personal or business purposes, but unauthorized redistribution or resale to third parties is prohibited.
Terms.art8Intro: We are not responsible for the following.
Terms.art8Item1: Any warranty regarding the quality, accuracy, or usefulness of listed content
Terms.art8Item2: Disputes arising from transactions between users
Terms.art8Item3: Damages caused by events beyond our reasonable control, such as natural disasters, communication failures, or cyberattacks
Terms.art8Item4: Damages resulting from temporary suspension or termination of the Service
Terms.art9Body: We may change or terminate the Service upon prior notice to users. In unavoidable circumstances, changes or suspension may occur without prior notice.
Terms.art10Body: These Terms are governed by the laws of Japan. The Tokyo District Court shall have exclusive jurisdiction as the court of first instance.
Terms.art11Body: We may amend these Terms as necessary. Amendments take effect when posted on the Service. Users who continue to use the Service after amendments are deemed to have accepted the amended Terms.

Home.heroDescription: The first knowledge marketplace where AI agents pay directly with SOL — via x402 autonomous payment. Human-in-the-loop mode also available.
Home.howItWorksSubtitle: AI agents autonomously discover and purchase knowledge via x402. Human-in-the-loop mode also supported — agent proposes, you approve.
Home.howItWorksStep1Desc: Add the MCP server to Claude Code or Cursor. Just a few lines of JSON config.
Home.howItWorksStep2Desc: Use km_search to find knowledge from the marketplace. Evaluate by quality score and trust rating.
Home.howItWorksStep3Desc: Agent pays directly via HTTP 402 protocol, sending SOL and building payment proof. Human-in-the-loop approval mode also supported.
Home.howItWorksStep4Desc: Access full content immediately after purchase. Integrates directly into the agent's workflow.
Home.heroCatchphrase: The era when AI agents autonomously purchase knowledge
Home.valueProp1Desc: AI agents pay directly with SOL via x402. No human approval required.
Home.valueProp2Desc: Sub-second payments via HTTP 402. Zero intermediaries.
Home.valueProp3Desc: We never hold private keys. Direct wallet-to-wallet.
Home.finalCtaSellerDesc: List prompts, datasets, and know-how. AI agents discover and purchase autonomously.
Home.finalCtaAgentDesc: Supports Claude Code, Cursor, ElizaOS, AgentKit. Agents purchase knowledge autonomously with just a few lines of config.
Home.accessMethod1Desc: Just add to Claude Code / Cursor config. Agent autonomously searches and buys knowledge.
Home.accessMethod2Desc: Buy and publish directly from the terminal. Integrates into scripts and CI/CD pipelines.
Home.accessMethod3Desc: Full control via HTTP. Integrate into any agent framework or custom application.
Home.definitionLine1: KnowMint is the first AI-native knowledge marketplace. AI agents autonomously discover, evaluate, and purchase human expertise — tacit knowledge, experiential insights, and specialized know-how — using the x402 protocol on Solana.
Home.definitionLine2: Payments flow directly from agent wallets to seller wallets in SOL. No intermediary holds funds or private keys. Human-in-the-loop mode is also supported: agents propose, humans approve.
Home.definitionLine3: Access via three interfaces: Web UI for browsing and listing, CLI (km) for terminal workflows, and MCP Server for seamless AI agent integration with Claude Code, Cursor, ElizaOS, and Coinbase AgentKit.

FAQ.title: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.description: Common questions about KnowMint, x402 payments, and AI agent integration
FAQ.a1: x402 is an open protocol by Coinbase that enables machine-to-machine payments via HTTP 402 status codes. When an AI agent requests paid content, the server responds with HTTP 402 and payment instructions. The agent then sends cryptocurrency (SOL on Solana) directly and resubmits the request with a payment proof. No human intervention required.
FAQ.q2: How do AI agents purchase knowledge on KnowMint?
FAQ.a2: AI agents use the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server to interact with KnowMint. The agent calls km_search to discover knowledge, km_get_detail to evaluate it, and km_get_content to purchase. When payment is required, the server returns HTTP 402 with the seller's wallet address and price. The agent sends SOL directly from its wallet and includes the transaction hash as payment proof.
FAQ.a3: KnowMint currently supports SOL payments on the Solana blockchain. Payments are direct wallet-to-wallet transfers — we never hold your funds or private keys. Support for additional tokens (USDC) and EVM chains is planned.
FAQ.q4: How do I set up the MCP server for Claude Code or Cursor?
FAQ.a4: Add the following to your ~/.claude/mcp.json (Claude Code) or .cursor/mcp.json (Cursor): set the command to 'npx', args to ['--yes', '--package', '@knowmint/mcp-server@latest', 'mcp-server'], and provide your KM_API_KEY and KM_BASE_URL as environment variables. Get your API key from the Dashboard > API Keys page.
FAQ.a5: Yes. KnowMint is fully non-custodial — we never store or have access to your private keys. All payments are direct peer-to-peer transfers from the buyer's wallet to the seller's wallet. We only verify and record transaction hashes on the Solana blockchain.
FAQ.q6: What types of knowledge can I sell?
FAQ.a6: You can sell prompts, tool definitions and MCP configurations, datasets, API specifications, and general knowledge. Content is text-based and can include Markdown formatting. Each listing includes a preview (visible before purchase) and full content (accessible after purchase).
FAQ.a7: Due to the nature of digital content, refunds are generally not available once content has been disclosed. However, if the content materially differs from the description, a technical issue prevents access, or the seller provided false descriptions, we will work with the seller to find an appropriate resolution. Please review listings carefully before purchasing.
FAQ.q8: What are Solana gas fees and how much do they cost?
FAQ.a8: Solana gas fees are network transaction fees required to process blockchain transactions. On Solana, these fees are extremely low — typically less than $0.01 per transaction. The fee is paid by the buyer's wallet automatically when sending SOL to the seller. No additional platform fees are charged by KnowMint.
FAQ.q9: My purchase didn't go through. What should I do?
FAQ.a9: First, check your wallet for the transaction status. If the transaction was confirmed on-chain but content wasn't delivered, try refreshing the page or accessing the content again from your Library. If the issue persists, report it on our GitHub Issues page with your transaction hash — we can verify the on-chain transaction and resolve access issues.
FAQ.q10: How is my data handled on KnowMint?
FAQ.a10: We collect only what's necessary: email for authentication, wallet address for payments, and transaction hashes for verification. API keys are stored as SHA-256 hashes (plaintext is never stored). We don't share data with third-party advertising networks. All data is protected by Row Level Security (RLS) in our database. See our Privacy Policy for full details.
FAQ.a11: KnowMint supports any AI agent that can make HTTP requests. We provide official integrations for: MCP Server (Claude Code, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible client), ElizaOS Plugin (@knowledge-market/eliza-plugin), and Coinbase AgentKit ActionProvider (@knowledge-market/agentkit-plugin). You can also integrate via the REST API or CLI (km) tool.
FAQ.q12: How does KnowMint protect against fraud?
FAQ.a12: Multiple layers: all transactions are verified on the Solana blockchain, API keys use SHA-256 hashing, rate limiting prevents abuse (120 req/min pre-auth, 60 req/min per key), content reports are reviewed by administrators, and SECURITY DEFINER functions with restricted search paths protect database operations. The platform is also fully non-custodial — we never hold funds.
FAQ.a13: KnowMint is the first AI-native knowledge marketplace where AI agents autonomously discover and purchase human expertise using the x402 protocol on Solana. Humans list their tacit knowledge, experiential insights, and specialized expertise. AI agents find and buy this knowledge — paying SOL directly from their wallets to sellers' wallets with no intermediary.
FAQ.q14: How do I list knowledge on KnowMint?
FAQ.a14: Sign in, click 'List Item' in the navigation, fill in title, description, category, and price (in SOL), then add preview content (visible before purchase) and full content (accessible after purchase). You can save as draft or publish immediately. Content supports Markdown formatting.
FAQ.a15: No. KnowMint currently charges zero platform commission. The full payment goes directly from the buyer's wallet to the seller's wallet. The only additional cost is the Solana network transaction fee (gas), which is typically less than $0.01.
FAQ.q16: How do I use the CLI tool?
FAQ.a16: Install with 'npm run km -- <command>' or use npx. Available commands include: 'km search' to find knowledge, 'km install <id>' to purchase and download, 'km publish prompt <file> --price 0.1SOL' to list knowledge, and 'km my purchases' to view your purchases. Configure your API key in ~/.km/config.json.
FAQ.q17: How do I set up the MCP server with Claude Code or Cursor?
FAQ.a17: Add the KnowMint MCP server to your ~/.claude/mcp.json (Claude Code) or .cursor/mcp.json (Cursor). Set command to 'npx', args to ['--yes', '--package', '@knowmint/mcp-server@latest', 'mcp-server'], and add KM_API_KEY and KM_BASE_URL (https://knowmint.shop) as environment variables. Get your API key from Dashboard > API Keys.
FAQ.a18: KnowMint uses the Solana blockchain for all payments. Solana was chosen for its low transaction fees (typically < $0.01), fast confirmation times, and dominant position in AI agent transactions (77% of AI agent TX on Solana). Payments are made in SOL, the native token of Solana.
FAQ.a19: Yes. KnowMint is fully open source under the MIT License. The entire codebase — including the marketplace, MCP server, CLI, ElizaOS plugin, and AgentKit plugin — is available on GitHub at github.com/Sou0327/knowmint. Contributions are welcome.
FAQ.q20: How do I get an API key?
FAQ.a20: Sign in to KnowMint, go to Dashboard > API Keys, and click 'Create new API key'. Choose a name, select permissions (read, write, or admin), and create. The key is shown only once — copy it immediately. API keys are stored as SHA-256 hashes; we cannot retrieve your key later.

Developers.description: Integrate KnowMint into your AI agent workflow via MCP Server, CLI, or REST API
Developers.heroDesc: 3 ways to integrate: MCP Server for AI agents, CLI for terminal workflows, and REST API for full control.
Developers.mcpDesc: Add the MCP server to Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible client. Your AI agent will autonomously search and purchase knowledge.

Developers.mcpProse: The KnowMint MCP server (@knowmint/mcp-server) lets AI agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible clients autonomously search, evaluate, and purchase knowledge from the marketplace. Once configured, agents can discover relevant expertise and complete purchases using the x402 protocol without human intervention.
Developers.cliProse: The KnowMint CLI (km) is a standalone Node.js command-line tool for searching, purchasing, and publishing knowledge directly from your terminal. It supports automated deployment of purchased knowledge to Claude Code and OpenCode configurations.
Developers.apiProse: The KnowMint REST API provides programmatic access to all marketplace operations. It uses API key authentication (SHA-256 hashed), supports the x402 payment protocol for content access, and can be integrated into any agent framework or custom application.

About.title: About KnowMint
About.description: Learn about KnowMint — the first AI-native knowledge marketplace using x402 on Solana
About.missionBody: KnowMint exists to bridge the gap between human tacit knowledge and AI agents. We believe that experiential insights, specialized expertise, and practical know-how should be discoverable and purchasable by AI systems — enabling agents to autonomously augment their capabilities with real-world knowledge.
About.whatBody: KnowMint is the first AI-native knowledge marketplace where AI agents autonomously discover and purchase human expertise using the x402 protocol on Solana. Humans list their tacit knowledge, experiential insights, and specialized expertise. AI agents find and buy this knowledge through MCP servers, CLI, or REST API — paying directly from their wallets to sellers' wallets. No intermediary holds funds or private keys.
About.teamBody: KnowMint is built and maintained by a solo founder. The entire platform — from frontend to blockchain integration, security hardening to AI agent SDKs — was developed using AI-assisted coding (Claude Code). This project itself is a proof of concept: what one person can build when human expertise meets AI capabilities.
About.openSourceBody: KnowMint is fully open source under the MIT License. We believe in transparency and invite contributions from the community.
About.storyBody1: KnowMint was born from a simple observation: AI agents are increasingly capable of executing complex tasks, but they lack access to the tacit knowledge that humans accumulate through years of experience. Prompt libraries exist, but they're static. AI training data is vast, but it doesn't capture the nuanced, situational expertise that professionals develop over careers.
About.storyBody2: We asked: what if AI agents could autonomously find and purchase the exact expertise they need, directly from the humans who have it? What if this transaction could happen instantly, without intermediaries, using cryptocurrency? The result is KnowMint — where human knowledge meets autonomous AI purchasing power.
About.whySolanaBody: Solana processes 77% of all AI agent transactions. Its sub-second finality and near-zero fees (typically less than $0.01 per transaction) make it ideal for micro-transactions between AI agents and knowledge sellers. When an agent needs a $0.50 prompt template, paying $15 in Ethereum gas fees makes no sense. Solana makes micro-knowledge-commerce viable.
About.whyX402Body: The x402 protocol, created by Coinbase, brings native payment capabilities to HTTP — the language AI agents already speak. When an agent requests paid content, the server returns HTTP 402 with payment instructions. The agent pays autonomously and resubmits. No OAuth flows, no subscription management, no billing APIs. Just HTTP request, pay, and receive. This is how machines should transact.
About.whyMcpBody: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is becoming the standard for AI agent tool integration. By providing a native MCP server, KnowMint becomes a first-class tool in any MCP-compatible AI workflow — Claude Code, Cursor, and beyond. Agents don't need custom integrations; they use KnowMint like any other tool in their toolkit.
About.roadmapItem3: Knowledge quality scoring and verification system
About.value1Body: AI agents should be able to acquire knowledge without human intervention. Every design decision prioritizes autonomous agent workflows.
About.value2Body: We never hold private keys or funds. Every transaction is a direct peer-to-peer transfer. Your keys, your crypto, your knowledge.
About.value3Body: Our entire codebase is MIT-licensed. The marketplace, MCP server, CLI, and all SDKs are open source. Transparency builds trust.
About.value4Body: AI is powerful, but human experience is irreplaceable. KnowMint exists because tacit knowledge — the kind you can only get from doing — has immense value that deserves a marketplace.

Security.title: Security
Security.description: KnowMint security practices — non-custodial architecture, encryption, and audit history
Security.introBody: Security is foundational to KnowMint. As a marketplace handling cryptocurrency payments, we implement defense-in-depth across every layer.
Security.nonCustodialBody: KnowMint never holds your private keys or funds. All payments are direct peer-to-peer transfers from buyer wallets to seller wallets on Solana. We only verify and record transaction hashes.
Security.apiKeysBody: API keys are hashed with SHA-256 before storage. Plaintext keys are never stored or logged. Keys are shown once at creation and cannot be retrieved afterward.
Security.headersBody: All responses include HSTS (max-age=63072000, includeSubDomains, preload), X-Frame-Options: DENY, Content-Security-Policy (no unsafe-eval), Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, and Permissions-Policy restricting camera, microphone, and geolocation.
Security.authBody: Wallet connections use Sign-In with Solana (SIWS). Challenges are generated server-side, consumed atomically via SECURITY DEFINER RPC, and verified using Ed25519 signature validation.
Security.rateLimitBody: Multi-layer rate limiting protects against abuse: 120 req/min per IP (pre-auth), 60 req/min per API key (authenticated), 20 req/min for auth endpoints. Exceeded limits return HTTP 429 with X-RateLimit-Remaining and X-RateLimit-Reset headers.
Security.dbBody: All database tables are protected by Row-Level Security (RLS). Sensitive operations use SECURITY DEFINER functions with restricted search_path (pg_catalog, public). Service-role functions are explicitly revoked from anon and authenticated roles.
Security.auditBody: The codebase has undergone 13+ rounds of automated security review (Codex), plus manual Black/White Hacker dual-agent penetration testing. Each round identified and resolved vulnerabilities until zero issues remained.
Security.disclosureBody: If you discover a security vulnerability, please report it via GitHub Issues or email us at contact@knowmint.shop. We take all reports seriously and will respond promptly.
